mission.title: helm: Fail strict lints when unused values are provided
mission.description:
**What this PR does / why we need it**:

When an unused value is passed into the chart, a strict lint will throw an error. For example, given the chart, a `helm lint --strict` will fail.

```
# templates/configmap.yaml
kind: ConfigMap
apiVersion: v1
metadata:
  name: example-chart
  namespace: example-chart
  labels:
    team: infrastructure
    chart_name: {{ $.Chart.Name }}
    chart_version: {{ $.Chart.Version }}
  annotations:
    deleteme: "if found"
```

```
# values.yaml
super:
  unused:

steps.title: Verify the fix
steps.description: Confirm that the issue is resolved in your environment by testing the affected functionality.

resolution.summary:
@drewgonzales360 the solution you built for your company, is that something that is shareable or is it a very custom fit solution?

For example i did a verrry rough POC in a bash script that pulls a helms values.yaml tturns them into the `x.y.z` key format  and diffs against the values.yaml that we provide in the same format

It works... but there are assumptions around the remote values.yaml being correct and all object keys being implemented which is almost always never fully filled out.

Was just curious if your approach was any cleaner/smarter/robust? 

(for anyone curious)
i.e

```
#!/bin/bash
file1=$(mktemp)
file2=$(mktemp)
keys1=$(mktemp)
keys2=$(mktemp)

wget https://raw.githubusercontent.com/argoproj/argo-helm/main/charts/argo-rollouts/values.yaml -O "$file1"
cat ./values.yaml >"$file2"

grep -v -E '^[[:space:]]*(-|#)' "$file1" | grep -v '^$' >"$file1.tmp" && mv "$file1.tmp" "$file1"
grep -v -E '^[[:space:]]*(-|#)' "$file2" | grep -v '^$' >"$file2.tmp" && mv "$file2.tmp" "$file2"

# Recursive function to print full paths of all keys
function traverse() {
    local file=$1
    local path=$2
    echo "$path" | tr -d "-" | sed 's/\.$//'
    local keys=$(yq e ".${path} | keys" "$file" 2>/dev/null)
    for key in $keys; do
        traverse "$file" "${path}.${key}"
    done
}

# Generate lists of keys for both files

keys=$(yq e 'keys' "$file1")
for key in $keys; do
    traverse "$file1" "$key" >>"$keys1"
done
cat "$keys1" | sort | uniq >"$keys1.tmp" && mv "$keys1.tmp" "$k
codeSnippets:
# templates/configmap.yaml
kind: ConfigMap
apiVersion: v1
metadata:
  name: example-chart
  namespace: example-chart
  labels:
    team: infrastructure
    chart_name: {{ $.Chart.Name }}
    chart_version: {{ $.Chart.Version }}
  annotations:
    deleteme: "if found"

# templates/configmap.yaml
kind: ConfigMap
apiVersion: v1
metadata:
  name: example-chart
  namespace: example-chart
  labels:
    team: infrastructure
    chart_name: {{ $.Chart.Name }}
    chart_version: {{ $.Chart.Version }}
  annotations:
    deleteme: "if found"

# values.yaml
super:
  unused: REQUIRED
  value:
    that: REQUIRED
    is_not: REQUIRED
  used:
    ever: REQUIRED

❯ /usr/local/bin/helm lint --strict example-chart
==> Linting example-chart
[INFO] Chart.yaml: icon is recommended
[ERROR] templates/: there are unused fields in values files [.Values.super.unused .Values.super.used.ever .Values.super.value.is_not .Values.super.value.that]

Error: 1 chart(s) linted, 1 chart(s) failed

usedValues = sets.Set[string]()
providedValues = sets.Set[string]()
for every file in the chart
  usedValues.insert( all values referenced in the file )
  providedValues.insert( all values provided )
end

unusedValues = setDifference(usedValues, providedValues)
return unusedValues

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with helm installed or the issue environment reproducible.
